title: Agent engineering path handout (modules 10–12)
subtitle: Agent cycle, 3A model, and workflow template – use it when planning one agentic process.
agentCycleSteps: Goal – define what result the agent must produce and when it must ask a human.
Context – provide data, sources, boundaries, and quality criteria.
Actions – split the work into clear steps: plan, act, check, improve.
Observation – record what failed, what data was missing, and where human judgment was needed.
Update – after a test, improve the rule, prompt, skill, or test case.
threeAModel: Automatize – a rule-based flow when most steps repeat.
Augment – AI helps the human, but the human approves important decisions.
Autonomize – a limited-autonomy agent acts on its own, but has clear boundaries and escalation.
workflowSteps: Trigger – what starts the process: form, email, table, file, or manual action.
Action – what the system does: gathers information, prepares a summary, creates a task.
Tool – which tool it uses: search, API, spreadsheet, CRM, Teams, or Slack.
Human gate – where the human approves, rejects, or asks for a revision.
Log – where the result, error, and lesson for next time are stored.
promptPatterns: Coordinator: split the task into roles, steps, and handoff rules.
Specialist: do one clear part, use the provided sources, and do not guess when data is missing.
Evaluator: check facts, risks, tone, and whether the criteria were answered.
Failure rule: if data is missing or a tool fails, write "Failed" and state the reason.
reflectionSummary: Within 24–48 hours, choose one process and run a small agent chain: coordinator → specialist → evaluator. Record one lesson and update the prompt.
nextPageIntro: The Agent path is complete when you have one repeatable process: a diagram, prompts, a test case, and a clear human approval point.
footerText: Prompt Anatomy – prompt structure training. © Course material.
